exercises.title: Default Parameter Values
exercises.description: Default values make arguments optional. Call the function with and without the optional parameters to see defaults in action.
exercises.starterCode:
def greet(name: str, greeting: str = "Hello", punctuation: str = "!") -> str:
    """Return a greeting string."""
    return f"{greeting}, {name}{punctuation}"

# TODO: Call with all three arguments
print(greet("Alice", "Hi", "."))

# TODO: Call with just name and greeting (use default punctuation)
print(greet("Bob", "Good morning"))

# TODO: Call with just the name (use both defaults)
print(greet("Carol"))

# TODO: Override only the punctuation using keyword argument
print(greet("Dave", punctuation="?"))
exercises.expectedOutput:
Hi, Alice.
Good morning, Bob!
Hello, Carol!
Hello, Dave?
hints: Passing a value overrides the default; omitting it uses the default.
Use keyword arguments to override non-final defaults: greet('Dave', punctuation='?')

exercises.title: *args: Variable Positional Arguments
exercises.description: *args collects any number of positional arguments into a tuple.
exercises.starterCode:
def total(*amounts: float) -> float:
    """Sum any number of amounts."""
    result = 0
    for amount in amounts:
        result += amount
    return result

# Call with different numbers of arguments
print(total(10, 20))
print(total(5, 15, 25, 35))
print(total(100))
print(total())
hints: *amounts receives all positional arguments as a tuple.
An empty call total() gives an empty tuple, so the sum is 0.

exercises.title: **kwargs: Variable Keyword Arguments
exercises.description: **kwargs collects any number of keyword arguments into a dictionary.
exercises.starterCode:
def create_profile(name: str, **details) -> None:
    """Print a profile with any extra keyword details."""
    print(f"--- {name}'s Profile ---")
    for key, value in details.items():
        print(f"  {key}: {value}")

# Call with different keyword arguments each time
create_profile("Alice", age=28, city="Tokyo", hobby="painting")
print()
create_profile("Bob", role="Developer", company="Python Inc")
exercises.expectedOutput:
--- Alice's Profile ---
  age: 28
  city: Tokyo
  hobby: painting

--- Bob's Profile ---
  role: Developer
  company: Python Inc
hints: **details captures all extra keyword arguments as a dict.
details.items() gives (key, value) pairs for iteration.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Default Arguments
warmup.description: Default parameters must come AFTER non-default parameters.
warmup.starterCode:
def power(base, exponent=2):
    """Raise base to exponent (default square)."""
    return base ** exponent

print(power(5))     # uses default exponent=2
print(power(5, 3))  # overrides default
print(power(2, 10))
